Title: Verify Flash activity is displayed upon system startup
Test Steps:
1. Launch the system
2. Observe the Flash activity is displayed

Title: Verify user can register with valid credentials
Test Data:
- Valid username: testuser1
- Valid email: testuser1@example.com
- Valid password: Test1234
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Register" button in the Flash activity
2. Enter the valid username, email, and password
3. Click the "Register" button
4. Observe the system redirects to the Login activity
5. Observe the user's registration is saved in the Postgresol database

Title: Verify user can log in with valid credentials
Test Data:
- Valid username: testuser1
- Valid password: Test1234
Test Steps:
1. Enter the valid username and password in the Login activity
2. Click the "Login" button
3. Observe the system redirects to the Newsletter activity

Title: Verify user can view newsletters
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Newsletter activity
2. Observe the newsletters are displayed

Title: Verify user can create a new event
Test Data:
- Event title: Test Event 1
- Event description: This is a test event
- Event date: 2023-05-01
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Create event activity
2. Enter the event title, description, and date
3. Click the "Create" button
4. Observe the system redirects to the Join/view event activity
5. Observe the new event is displayed

Title: Verify user can join or view an existing event
Preconditions: User has logged in and created an event
Test Data: - Event ID: 1 (the event created in TC-Create-Event-001)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Join/view event activity
2. Enter the event ID
3. Click the "Join" or "View" button (depending on the event status)
4. Observe the event details are displayed

Title: Verify user can view their profile
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Profile activity
2. Observe the user's profile details are displayed

Title: Verify user can log out
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Logout" button
2. Observe the system redirects to the Flash activity

Title: Verify data is saved and retrieved from the Postgresol database
Preconditions: User has registered and created an event
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve user data from the Postgresol database
2. Retrieve event data from the Postgresol database
3. Compare the retrieved data with the data entered during registration and event creation
4. Observe the data matches

Title: Verify notifications are displayed in the notification center
Preconditions: User has logged in and created an event
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Notification center
2. Observe notifications related to user registration, event creation, and event updates are displayed

Title: Verify system handles invalid inputs
Module: Register, Login, Create event, Join/view event
Test Data:
- Invalid username: testuser123 (too long)
- Invalid email: testuser1@example.com.invalid (invalid domain)
- Invalid password: Test123 (too short)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to register, log in, create an event, or join/view an event with invalid inputs
2. Observe error messages are displayed

Title: Verify system handles edge cases
Module: Register, Login, Create event, Join/view event
Test Data:
- Empty username, email, or password during registration
- Existing username during registration
- Existing email during registration
- Empty event title, description, or date during event creation
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to register, log in, create an event, or join/view an event with edge cases
2. Observe error messages are displayed or appropriate actions are taken (e.g., existing username or email should not be allowed during registration)
